2954.name: Cookery
2954.description: The loathsome smell of rotten fish fills the air but doesn't manage to completely mask another stench that is even less pleasant, and somehow frightening. A large iron cauldron sits in the center of the room, an eerie green flame flickering at its base.

npcs.name: Grulla the hag
npcs.description: She smells strongly of rotten fish and wears no clothing on her lanky body.  Her hair and skin is a dirty green color and her mouth is just a

2955.name: Alchemy Lab
2955.description: Braziers emit pungent clouds of smoke and small, iron bowls boil over tiny fires. A table rests against the south wall, its surface cluttered with various bric-a-brac.

npcs.name: Heptis the hag
npcs.description: She smells strongly of rotten fish and wears no clothing on her lanky body.  Her hair and skin is a dirty green color and her mouth is just a

2956.name: Scryery
2956.description: Dead fish, in various stages of decomposition, are scattered about the room. A large stone slab occupies the center of the room, its surface littered with fish entrails.

npcs.name: Dregla the hag
npcs.description: She smells strongly of rotten fish and wears no clothing on her lanky body.  Her hair and skin is a dirty green color and her mouth is just a

2957.name: Chamber of Tears
2957.description: The walls of this chamber are made of a strange porous stone. A clear liquid continuously forces its way out of the walls and collects into large droplets which slide down the wall's surface, like large tears, and are collected in a small basin.

2958.name: Smooth Passage
2958.description: The floor of this passage is very smooth, as if polished by the passage of many feet over eons of time. The passage heads off to the north, small chambers are to the east and west.

2959.name: Funnel`s Mouth
2959.description: This room is shaped like a funnel resting on its side. The room narrows to the north where a dark doorway leads to another room from which you hear the sound of water in violent motion. Another doorway is to the west.
